name: Lily of the Valley
description: Lily of the Valley (Convallaria majalis) is one of the most dangerous plants for cats. Despite its delicate appearance, every part — flowers, leaves, stems, berries, and even the water in a vase — contains potent cardiac glycosides including convallatoxin, convalloside, and over 30 related compounds. These affect the heart in a manner similar to Foxglove, and even tiny amounts can be lethal.
safetyNotes: Toxic compounds: convallatoxin, convalloside, and 30+ related cardiac glycosides; highest in flowers and berries
Mechanism: cardiac glycosides inhibit Na+/K+-ATPase in heart muscle, disrupting electrical conduction and causing severe arrhythmias
Symptoms: vomiting, drooling, bradycardia (dangerously slow heart rate), hypotension, cardiac arrhythmias, weakness, collapse; can be fatal
All plant parts are hazardous — even the water in a flower vase can cause toxicity
Any ingestion is a veterinary emergency; do not wait for symptoms to develop

symptoms.name: Vomiting, diarrhea (which may contain blood), abdominal pain/discomfort, and trembling
symptoms.onset: Variable latent period depending on the dose;
symptoms.notes: These symptoms are primarily triggered by the irritant saponins found throughout the plant12. The severe irritation of the gastrointestinal tract often precedes more dangerous systemic cardiovascular issues1. 2. Cardiovascular Depression and Arrhythmias

symptoms.name: Bradycardia (a sudden, significant decrease in heart rate) and severe cardiac arrhythmias (such as Atrioventricular or AV block)
symptoms.onset: Variable latent period depending on the dose;
symptoms.notes: This is the most dangerous consequence of Lily of the Valley toxicity and is caused by the plant's cardiac glycosides2.... These toxins alter how the cardiac muscle contracts and disrupt electrical conduction57. In acute cases, this can also lead to hyperkalemia (dangerously high potassium levels in

symptoms.name: Seizures and tremors
symptoms.onset: Variable; usually develops as the cardiovascular depression worsens
symptoms.notes: Seizures and trembling are secondary clinical signs that manifest as the toxins profoundly depress the cardiovascular system and disrupt cellular functions15.
